summary: Exact HEAD 3da7bb249c8ac1212f53f26bf06432fb1d7d6071 is safe for the staged PR-ready handoff. The public guarded command composes the production run-session owner, managed-worktree implementation, current-HEAD verification, independent read-only review, bounded needs_changes repair, and final Gate recheck. It cannot create or merge a PR, grant a waiver, deploy, publish, or perform another material external side effect. Compatibility remains explicit through the legacy profile and auditable autonomous-disable fallback; unavailable providers, missing capabilities, quota, permission, timeout, CI pending, cancellation, no progress, review block, and exhausted repair convergence remain typed non-green stops. There is no data migration or deployment surface, predecessor connector/review owners are reused, and target architecture conformance improves from 81 baseline violations to 80 without a run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency.
inspection_summary: Inspected the exact committed diff and worktree state; the gate release-risk request; Story, Architecture, Spec, and test plan contracts; public CLI wiring and help; the autonomous action registry, run-session composition, runtime adapter/connectors, independent review lifecycle, repair/current-HEAD rebind paths; current unit, integration, public E2E, typecheck, post-freeze targeted validation, adjudication, runtime-contract, architecture, and conformance artifacts. Classified the sole uncommitted .vibepro/config.json change as a temporary review-budget policy sync outside the committed release surface.
inspection_inputs: git diff origin/main...HEAD -- src/agent-runtime-adapter.js src/agent-runtime-connectors.js src/independent-review-orchestrator.js src/safe-action-orchestrator.js src/cli.js

findings.detail: The literal-only acceptance marker near the end of test/e2e/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure-runtime.spec.ts still describes the superseded post-PR CI/execute-merge closure wording, while the executable S-005 status assertion permits active or completed. This does not weaken the exact-HEAD release verdict: the canonical Story, Architecture, and Spec define staged pre-PR closure, both current SSOT statuses are active, the action registry excludes PR/merge/waiver, and post-PR delivery remains separately tracked. Align the marker during the focused post-PR closure update to avoid misleading future mechanical readers.

judgment_delta: Initial concern: the one-command path could expand release authority into PR creation, merge, waiver, deployment, publication, or another external effect -> Final conclusion: the closed autonomous registry contains only diagnose, prepare_artifacts, implement, verify, review, repair, and final_prepare; CLI help and Story/Architecture contracts explicitly retain all external release authority for a human command.

Initial concern: a successful happy path could hide unsafe fallback and failure behavior -> Final conclusion: exact-head coverage includes provider unavailability and capability loss before mutation, permission/quota/timeout, no progress, verification failure, review block, needs_changes repair, bounded convergence, CI pending, cancellation containment, stale runtime completion, and typed resume behavior.

Initial concern: repair could ship stale verification or stale independent review -> Final conclusion: a successful repair may replay only from verify, the suffix is forcibly re-executed, the selected Run is reloaded immediately before review, the managed-worktree HEAD is authoritative, and final_prepare rejects any pr-prepare artifact not bound to that HEAD.

Initial concern: the release could rely on test-only action-runner injection -> Final conclusion: public E2E scenario S-001 invokes runCli execute run without custom implementation, repair, or review action runners and observes real managed-worktree implementation and repair commits, separate read-only review lifecycles with needs_changes then pass, and current-HEAD final preparation.

Initial concern under regression_guard: making autonomous closure the default could silently break existing operators and persisted Runs -> Final conclusion: explicit --action-profile legacy remains supported, --disable-autonomous-actions records the autonomous-to-legacy resolution, existing persisted profiles remain authoritative, and the current 287-test post-freeze set includes legacy, resume, scope, connector, and session-efficiency regressions.

Initial concern under path_surface_coverage: CLI, JSON/human output, managed Run state, connector prompts, review records, Gate artifacts, and docs could express different authority boundaries -> Final conclusion: these surfaces consistently preserve current-HEAD readiness and typed stops, keep review read-only and identity-separated, and exclude external release operations; predecessor connector and review owners are composed rather than duplicated.

Initial concern: the change might require rollout ordering, schema migration, or deployment coordination -> Final conclusion: no package, database schema, migration, deployment workflow, or runtime service configuration is changed; rollout is a repository CLI behavior guarded by persisted action-profile compatibility and reversible default selection.

Initial concern: architecture expansion could raise the existing baseline risk -> Final conclusion: exact-head conformance reports 80 violations versus origin/main's 81, one fewer orphan, and no new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency.

Initial concern: the stale evidence-reuse section and older pr-prepare snapshot could be mistaken for current readiness -> Final conclusion: this review independently inspected the exact current HEAD plus newer current-bound verification artifacts; the coordinator must record both final reviews and regenerate pr-prepare before PR creation, so the older snapshot is workflow input rather than a pass assertion.

Initial concern: the dirty worktree could conceal unreviewed release code -> Final conclusion: git status shows only .vibepro/config.json, whose diff is a bounded temporary review-budget amendment; it changes no committed runtime, documentation, test, migration, or deployment surface and must remain excluded from the PR surface.
